    <main>
        
<article class="single-page">
    <div class="container">
        <header class="page-header">
            <h1>Sonification: From Image to Sound - Experience Report on Manual and Automated Approaches</h1>
            <p class="article-meta">4 August 2025</p>
        </header>
        <div class="page-content markdown-content">
            <h1 id="sonification-from-image-to-sound---experience-report-on-manual-and-automated-approaches">Sonification: From Image to Sound - Experience Report on Manual and Automated Approaches</h1>
<p>Sonification, this fascinating technique that transforms visual data into sonic experiences, plays a central role in my transmedia projects. Having had the opportunity to experiment with this approach through <strong>Fluctus Planetarium</strong> and its interactive adaptation <strong>Fluctus Planetarium: The Game</strong>, I wish to share my experience with two complementary methodologies: manual sonification with ARGOPd/PureData for the original work and custom automation with Godot Engine for the interactive adaptation.</p>
<h2 id="sonification-in-my-creative-projects">Sonification in My Creative Projects</h2>
<p>As an electroacoustic composer, I have always been intrigued by the bridges between visual arts and sound creation. Sonification offers the unique possibility to make heard what is seen, to reveal the hidden musicality of shapes and colors. It was this curiosity that led me to integrate these techniques into <strong>Fluctus Planetarium</strong>, then to completely rethink the approach during its adaptation to an interactive game.</p>
<h2 id="manual-approach-fluctus-planetarium-and-argopd">Manual Approach: Fluctus Planetarium and ARGOPd</h2>
<h3 id="original-creative-context">Original Creative Context</h3>
<p>For <strong>Fluctus Planetarium</strong>, my acousmatic project, I needed to translate the <strong>planetary textures created by Louis Jeanne</strong> into sound material. These visual works, resulting from the deconstruction of portraits into abstract and organic textures, constitute the base material for my sonic creation.</p>
<h3 id="manual-vectorization-process-for-argopd">Manual Vectorization Process for ARGOPd</h3>
<p>The particularity of the <strong>ARGOPd Theme8 sonification module</strong> (developed by Gérard Paresys) that I used is that it requires <strong>vector graphic scores</strong> specifically formatted to function. The &ldquo;Score/Graphic Score&rdquo; module uses Pure Data&rsquo;s &ldquo;data structures&rdquo; and requires meticulous preparation of the visual material.</p>
<p>I therefore developed a <strong>manual vectorization</strong> process of Louis Jeanne&rsquo;s textures:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Analysis</strong> of the organic textures of the planets</li>
<li><strong>Extraction</strong> of significant graphic elements</li>
<li><strong>Manual vectorization</strong> into geometric shapes compatible with Pd data structures</li>
<li><strong>Creation</strong> of graphic scores exploitable by the Theme8 module</li>
</ol>
<div style="display: flex; gap: 20px; margin: 20px 0;">
  <div style="flex: 1;">
    <img src="/img/matis_texture.png" alt="Organic texture by Louis Jeanne" style="width: 100%; height: auto;" />
    <p style="text-align: center; font-style: italic; margin-top: 10px;">Organic texture by Louis Jeanne</p>
  </div>
  <div style="flex: 1;">
    <img src="/img/matis_partition.png" alt="Vectorized score for ARGOPd" style="width: 100%; height: auto;" />
    <p style="text-align: center; font-style: italic; margin-top: 10px;">Vectorized score for ARGOPd</p>
  </div>
</div>
<p><em>Visual comparison: The original organic texture by Louis Jeanne (gray and blue organic tones) and its manual vectorization into a colorful graphic score (bright geometric shapes) - same structural framework, different vectorial interpretation</em></p>
<p>This vectorization step is <strong>crucial</strong>: it transforms the fluid and organic textures into discrete geometric elements that the ARGOPd module can interpret as sound instructions.</p>
<h3 id="argopd-theme8-an-exceptional-tool-for-interpretation">ARGOPd Theme8: An Exceptional Tool for Interpretation</h3>
<p>Once the vectorized scores are created according to Theme8 specifications, <strong>ARGOPd</strong> reveals all its power. This suite of over 200 modules and 300 patches for PureData, co-winner of the 2014 AFIM International Musical Software Competition, offers particularly sophisticated <strong>sonification patches</strong>.</p>
<p>The Theme8 &ldquo;Score/Graphic Score&rdquo; module uses Pure Data&rsquo;s &ldquo;data structures&rdquo; and allows:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Sequential reading</strong> of the vectorized elements of the score</li>
<li><strong>Transformation</strong> of each geometric shape into sound parameters</li>
<li><strong>Artistic control</strong> of playback speed and rendering quality</li>
<li><strong>Generation</strong> of real-time audio streams from visual data</li>
</ul>
<p>Using vectorized scores in ARGOPd Theme8 allowed me to:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Artistically control</strong> every aspect of the visual-to-sound transposition</li>
<li><strong>Musically interpret</strong> Louis Jeanne&rsquo;s textures</li>
<li><strong>Compose</strong> customized processing chains</li>
<li><strong>Create</strong> an authentic dialogue between visual arts and electroacoustic composition</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="results-of-manual-sonification">Results of Manual Sonification</h3>
<p>The graphic scores I created reveal the <strong>creative mediation</strong> necessary between the original visual art and its sonification. The colorful geometric shapes - triangles, rectangles and polygons - combine with audio signal representations in the ARGOPd interface, creating a true <strong>contemporary score</strong> where each color and shape corresponds to specific sound parameters.</p>
<p>This manual approach offers exceptional <strong>creative flexibility</strong>. Each parameter can be adjusted intuitively, allowing exploration of different sonic interpretations of the original textures until the desired artistic result is achieved.</p>
<h2 id="automated-approach-fluctus-planetarium-the-game-and-godot-engine">Automated Approach: Fluctus Planetarium: The Game and Godot Engine</h2>
<h3 id="interactive-adaptation-challenge">Interactive Adaptation Challenge</h3>
<p>When adapting <strong>Fluctus Planetarium</strong> into the interactive version <strong>Fluctus Planetarium: The Game</strong>, the constraints were entirely different: I had to automate sonification to allow players to progressively discover musical creation. The goal was to develop an algorithm capable of automatically generating interesting sonic material from any image, without going through the manual vectorization step.</p>
<p>This transition from acousmatic work to interactive experience forced me to <strong>completely rethink</strong> the sonification approach, moving from manual artistic interpretation to direct pedagogical automation.</p>
<h3 id="custom-algorithmic-innovation">Custom Algorithmic Innovation</h3>
<p>I developed an original sonification system that directly analyzes pixels without prior vectorization:</p>
<h4 id="rgb-to-harmonics-mapping">RGB to Harmonics Mapping</h4>
<div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4"><code class="language-gdscript" data-lang="gdscript"><span style="color:#66d9ef">var</span> r_freq <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> lerp(<span style="color:#ae81ff">110.0</span>, <span style="color:#ae81ff">440.0</span>, color<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>r)    <span style="color:#75715e"># Red: 110-440 Hz</span>
<span style="color:#66d9ef">var</span> g_freq <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> lerp(<span style="color:#ae81ff">880.0</span>, <span style="color:#ae81ff">2200.0</span>, color<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>g)   <span style="color:#75715e"># Green: 880-2200 Hz  </span>
<span style="color:#66d9ef">var</span> b_freq <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> lerp(<span style="color:#ae81ff">4400.0</span>, <span style="color:#ae81ff">8800.0</span>, color<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>b)  <span style="color:#75715e"># Blue: 4400-8800 Hz</span>
</code></pre></div><h4 id="progressive-revelation-in-5-temporal-phases">Progressive Revelation in 5 Temporal Phases</h4>
<p>The algorithm progressively reveals sonic complexity over 10 seconds:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>0-2s</strong>: Red only</li>
<li><strong>2-4s</strong>: Blue only</li>
<li><strong>4-6s</strong>: Green only</li>
<li><strong>6-8s</strong>: Red + Blue</li>
<li><strong>8-10s</strong>: Complete RGB</li>
</ul>
<p>This pedagogical approach allows players to progressively understand how each color component contributes to the final sonic result, creating a <strong>bridge</strong> between the original work and its appropriation by users.</p>
<h4 id="professional-audio-quality">Professional Audio Quality</h4>
<ul>
<li><strong>Bilinear interpolation</strong> for smooth image traversal</li>
<li><strong>Temporal smoothing</strong> (factor 0.95) to reduce granularity</li>
<li><strong>Normalization to -1dB</strong> for optimal audio level</li>
<li><strong>Anti-click envelopes</strong> with fade in/out of 2000 samples</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="real-time-effects-chain">Real-Time Effects Chain</h3>
<p>The algorithm generates a <strong>AudioStreamWAV</strong> 44.1kHz 16-bit which is then processed by a complete effects chain:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>6-band Equalizer</strong> (Bass/Medium/Treble)</li>
<li><strong>Pitch Shift</strong>, <strong>Reverb</strong>, <strong>Delay</strong>, <strong>Chorus</strong>, <strong>Distortion</strong></li>
<li><strong>Real-time spectrum analyzer</strong> with 16-band VU meters</li>
<li><strong>Integrated oscilloscope</strong> with 512-sample buffer</li>
<li><strong>User-editable volume curve</strong></li>
</ul>
<p>This architecture transforms the sound workshop into a true <strong>virtual studio</strong> accessible to non-musician players, democratizing access to techniques used in the original work.</p>
<h2 id="comparison-of-both-approaches">Comparison of Both Approaches</h2>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th><strong>Aspect</strong></th>
<th><strong>ARGOPd Theme8 (Manual)</strong></th>
<th><strong>Godot (Automated)</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>Preparation</strong></td>
<td>Mandatory manual vectorization</td>
<td>Direct pixel analysis</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Interface</strong></td>
<td>Pure Data data structures</td>
<td>Structured GDScript code</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Control</strong></td>
<td>Artistic interpretation</td>
<td>Reproducible algorithm</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Real-time</strong></td>
<td>Yes, via PureData</td>
<td>Yes, with spectral analysis</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Source format</strong></td>
<td>Specific vector scores</td>
<td>Any raster images</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Target audience</strong></td>
<td>Electroacoustic composers</td>
<td>General public via gaming</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Creativity</strong></td>
<td>Subjective interpretation</td>
<td>Objective logic</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h2 id="work-evolution-from-creation-to-adaptation">Work Evolution: From Creation to Adaptation</h2>
<p>This experience showed me how a <strong>single work</strong> can require radically different technical approaches depending on its dissemination context:</p>
<h3 id="original-work-fluctus-planetarium">Original Work (Fluctus Planetarium)</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Creative interpretation</strong> of Louis Jeanne&rsquo;s textures via vectorization for ARGOPd Theme8</li>
<li><strong>Total artistic control</strong> by the composer with Pure Data data structures</li>
<li><strong>Sonification</strong> as collaborative creative process and creative mediation</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="interactive-adaptation-fluctus-planetarium-the-game">Interactive Adaptation (Fluctus Planetarium: The Game)</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Automated algorithm</strong> for universal accessibility without preparation</li>
<li><strong>Progressive pedagogy</strong> of direct pixel-by-pixel sonification</li>
<li><strong>Democratization</strong> without prior creative mediation or technical skills</li>
</ul>
<h2 id="complementary-methods">Complementary Methods</h2>
<p>These two approaches reveal the richness of the sonification process:</p>
<h3 id="manual-approach-argopd-theme8-favors">Manual approach (ARGOPd Theme8) favors:</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Creative interpretation</strong> of visual material via vectorization</li>
<li><strong>Artistic collaboration</strong> between visual and sonic creators</li>
<li><strong>In-depth aesthetic research</strong> with meticulous preparation</li>
<li><strong>Dialogue</strong> between visual arts and electroacoustic composition</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="automated-approach-godot-promotes">Automated approach (Godot) promotes:</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Universal accessibility</strong> to techniques without preparation</li>
<li><strong>Reproducibility</strong> of results with any image</li>
<li><strong>Progressive learning</strong> through direct experience</li>
<li><strong>Personal appropriation</strong> of concepts without technical barriers</li>
</ul>
<h2 id="future-prospects">Future Prospects</h2>
<p>This adaptation experience opens fascinating perspectives for the future of electroacoustic works. One can imagine <strong>transmedia ecosystems</strong> where:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Manual interpretation</strong> (ARGOPd) maintains its collaborative artistic dimension</li>
<li><strong>Automated algorithms</strong> (Godot) broaden the audience without mediation</li>
<li><strong>Artificial intelligence</strong> assists the transition between creative approaches</li>
<li>The <strong>community</strong> explores and extends the work via diversified tools</li>
</ul>
<h2 id="conclusion">Conclusion</h2>
<p><strong>Fluctus Planetarium</strong> and its adaptation perfectly illustrate the richness of sonification approaches. From manual vectorization of Louis Jeanne&rsquo;s textures for the ARGOPd Theme8 module to the direct algorithm on Godot, each method reveals different aspects of the creative process.</p>
<p>My experience with this dual approach has convinced me that the future of sonification lies in complementarity: manual creative interpretation for aesthetic research with specialized tools like ARGOPd, intelligent automation for transmission and appropriation via custom solutions.</p>
<p>Sonification thus becomes a <strong>mediation language</strong> with multiple facets, capable of revealing the hidden musicality of our visual environment according to different levels of reading and interaction, from in-depth artistic collaboration requiring creative vectorization to accessible playful experience with direct pixel analysis.</p>
<p><em>This article explores sonification techniques in the context of Fluctus Planetarium transmedia projects. To learn more about my other research in electroacoustic sound creation, check out <a href="/posts/">my other articles</a>.</em></p>

        </div>

<article class="single-page">
    <div class="container">
        <header class="page-header">
            <h1>How Music Adapts to Your Genealogy in Ascendance</h1>
            <p class="article-meta">28 June 2025</p>
        </header>
        <div class="page-content markdown-content">
            <p>Since the launch of <em>Ascendance</em> a few weeks ago, one question keeps coming up: how can music really adapt to a genealogy? Let&rsquo;s dive into the behind-the-scenes of this interactive musical innovation.</p>
<h2 id="what-is-a-gedcom-file">What is a GEDCOM file?</h2>
<p>Before exploring the musical magic of <em>Ascendance</em>, let&rsquo;s first understand what makes this experience possible: the GEDCOM file.</p>
<p><strong>GEDCOM</strong> (Genealogical Data Communication) is the standard format for genealogy files. It&rsquo;s a simple text file with the <code>.ged</code> extension that contains all the information about your family tree: names, birth and death dates, family relationships, family events.</p>
<h3 id="how-to-create-your-gedcom-file">How to create your GEDCOM file?</h3>
<p><strong>If you use genealogy software:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Ancestry, MyHeritage, Geneanet</strong>: Go to your tree settings and click &ldquo;Export&rdquo; or &ldquo;Download GEDCOM&rdquo;</li>
<li><strong>Family Tree Maker, Gramps, Legacy</strong>: Export option in the main menu</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>If you&rsquo;re starting from scratch:</strong></p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Create a free account on <a href="https://www.geneanet.org">Geneanet</a></strong></li>
<li>Enter your family information (parents, grandparents, etc.)</li>
<li><strong>Export your tree</strong>: Menu → Tools → Save/Export → Export a branch</li>
<li><strong>Important</strong>: Choose <strong>&ldquo;Ascending branch&rdquo;</strong> and <strong>&ldquo;0 degree of collaterals&rdquo;</strong> to export only your direct ancestors (without uncles, aunts, cousins)</li>
<li>Select <strong>GEDCOM</strong> format and download the file</li>
<li>Load this file into <em>Ascendance</em> to discover your family music!</li>
</ol>
<p><strong>Why only direct ascendants?</strong> <em>Ascendance</em> is designed to explore your direct ancestral lineage. Collateral branches (brothers, sisters, uncles, aunts) are not necessary and could disrupt the musical experience.</p>
<h2 id="a-musical-journey-in-three-movements">A musical journey in three movements</h2>
<p><em>Ascendance</em> structures your genealogical exploration into three distinct musical phases, each with its own sonic identity:</p>
<h3 id="1-the-warm-up-a-journey-through-the-centuries">1. The Warm-up: A Journey Through the Centuries</h3>
<p>Before diving into your family history, <em>Ascendance</em> offers you a journey through musical history. Representative samples from each era - Pierre Henry, Léo Delibes, Gregorian chant, Ambrosian music, melodies from ancient Greece - layer in random order.</p>
<p>This introduction creates a unique temporal atmosphere, preparing the listener to travel back in time to their most distant ancestors.</p>
<h3 id="2-the-outward-journey-each-ancestor-a-melody">2. The Outward Journey: Each Ancestor, a Melody</h3>
<p>This is where the real magic happens. Your time ship travels up the family tree, and <strong>each ancestor generates their own acousmatic melody</strong> composed of three symbolic sound objects:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>A kind of bell (death knell)</strong> ringing at the date of their death</li>
<li><strong>A kind of two-voice choir</strong> lasting throughout their lifetime</li>
<li><strong>A kind of trumpet</strong> marking their birth date</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Intelligent spatialization:</strong> Your position in the tree determines what you hear. Only ancestors within a 4-generation radius are audible, creating a realistic field of perception distributed in quadraphony.</p>
<p><strong>Harmonic evolution:</strong> With each generation ascended, the sound objects rise by a comma (ninth of a tone), creating an ascending musical progression that symbolizes your journey into the ascendance of the visited tree.</p>
<h3 id="3-the-return-journey-the-temporal-avalanche">3. The Return Journey: The Temporal Avalanche</h3>
<p>Once you reach the most distant known ancestor, the ship makes its U-turn and descends back to the present at a speed of 10 years per second.</p>
<p>The music changes dramatically: gone are the individual bells, trumpets, and choirs. In their place comes <strong>an avalanche of double notes descending chromatically</strong>, symbolizing the cascade of generations that preceded you.</p>
<p>The journey ends with <strong>a majestic final chord</strong> marking your return to the present.</p>
<h2 id="technical-innovation-in-service-of-emotion">Technical Innovation in Service of Emotion</h2>
<p>This approach revolutionizes musical listening by creating a <strong>unique soundtrack for each family</strong>. Your genealogical music depends entirely on:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Your tree&rsquo;s structure</strong> (number of ancestors, known generations)</li>
<li><strong>Historical dates</strong> of your ancestors (births, deaths)</li>
<li><strong>Your navigation</strong> through genealogical space</li>
<li><strong>The richness of your GEDCOM data</strong></li>
</ul>
<h2 id="a-deeply-personal-listening-experience">A Deeply Personal Listening Experience</h2>
<p><em>Ascendance</em> transforms genealogical exploration into a contemplative musical journey. Each family has its sonic signature, its rhythms, its harmonies. The work you hear will never exist for anyone else because it springs from your unique family history.</p>
<p>This approach questions our relationship to time, memory, and heritage, proposing a new form of transmission: one that passes through listening and electroacoustic musical emotion.</p>
<hr>
<p><em>Download Ascendance for free on <a href="https://jcploquin.itch.io/ascendance">itch.io</a> and discover the music hidden in your genealogy.</em></p>

        </div>
        
        
        
        <nav class="article-nav">
            
                <a href="/en/blog/quest-ce-que-lacousmatique/" class="prev-article">← What is Acousmatic Music?</a>

        </nav>
        
    </div>
</article>

    <main>
        
<article class="single-page">
    <div class="container">
        <header class="page-header">
            <h1>Acousmatic Music in Video Games</h1>
            <p class="article-meta">2 July 2025</p>
        </header>
        <div class="page-content markdown-content">
            <p>Long confined to concert halls and artistic installations, acousmatic music today finds an unexpected field of expression: video games. This encounter between electroacoustic tradition and digital interactivity opens fascinating perspectives for contemporary sound art.</p>
<h2 id="when-the-invisible-becomes-playable">When the Invisible Becomes Playable</h2>
<p>Acousmatic music, the art of pure listening freed from visual sources, shares a fundamental characteristic with video games: <strong>the creation of imaginary universes through sound</strong>. In my interactive works, this approach allows listeners to discover the richness of electroacoustic composition through an immersive artistic experience.</p>
<p><strong>Points of convergence:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Total immersion</strong>: The player dives into a sonic bath like the listener of an acousmatic work</li>
<li><strong>3D spatialization</strong>: Multichannel diffusion techniques find their equivalent in 3D audio for games</li>
<li><strong>Sonic narration</strong>: Soundscapes tell stories without words</li>
<li><strong>Contemplative interactivity</strong>: The player&rsquo;s action influences musical evolution</li>
</ul>
<h2 id="playing-music-an-artistic-revelation">&ldquo;Playing Music&rdquo;: An Artistic Revelation</h2>
<p>It was the expression <strong>&ldquo;playing music&rdquo;</strong> that opened this creative path for me. If one can &ldquo;play&rdquo; an instrument, why couldn&rsquo;t one &ldquo;play&rdquo; an electroacoustic composition? The video game medium, initially designed for entertainment, then becomes an <strong>artistic medium</strong> for my interactive experiments.</p>
<p>This approach radically transforms the relationship to the work: the listener becomes <strong>interpreter</strong> of their own musical experience, exploring the nuances and subtleties of acousmatic composition through their actions.</p>
<h2 id="the-legacy-of-electroacoustic-pioneers">The Legacy of Electroacoustic Pioneers</h2>
<p>Composers like Pierre Schaeffer or Denis Smalley developed sound morphology techniques that today find their application in my interactive creations. The notion of <strong>sound object</strong> becomes <strong>playable element</strong>, <strong>acousmatic diffusion</strong> transforms into <strong>immersive experience</strong>.</p>
<h2 id="experimenting-with-acousmatic-phrasing-the-example-of-music--me">Experimenting with Acousmatic Phrasing: The Example of Music &amp; Me</h2>
<p>In <em>Music &amp; Me</em>, the listener experiments with <strong>acousmatic phrasing</strong> through the chaining of more or less controllable sound object triggers. The work progresses pedagogically:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Solo</strong>: Learning to trigger and control individual sound objects</li>
<li><strong>Duo</strong>: Experimenting with interaction between two sound sources</li>
<li><strong>Trio</strong>: Developing polyphonic complexity</li>
<li><strong>Quartet</strong>: Mastering acousmatic polyphony and active listening</li>
</ul>
<p>This progression allows exploring the fundamentals of electroacoustic composition: articulation, sound morphology, and construction of musical discourse through object assembly.</p>
<h2 id="experimenting-with-spatialization-the-example-of-ascendance">Experimenting with Spatialization: The Example of Ascendance</h2>
<p>In <em>Ascendance</em>, it&rsquo;s <strong>spatialization</strong> that we experiment with, through moving the listening point in a sound space too large to be grasped all at once. The listener navigates in a genealogical universe where each ancestor generates their own sound objects - bells, choirs, trumpets - distributed in space according to family relationships.</p>
<p>This approach reveals the subtleties of <strong>acousmatic diffusion</strong>: sonic perspective, depth of field, and construction of space through sound.</p>
<h2 id="full-fledged-works-of-art">Full-Fledged Works of Art</h2>
<p><strong>My interactive creations are:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Electroacoustic compositions</strong> using interactivity as an expressive parameter</li>
<li><strong>Artistic experiences</strong> where listening becomes performance</li>
<li><strong>Unique works</strong> that question the boundaries between composer and interpreter</li>
<li><strong>Sound laboratories</strong> where each interaction reveals new musical aspects</li>
</ul>
<p><em>Music &amp; Me</em> and <em>Ascendance</em> are not &ldquo;musical games&rdquo; but <strong>interactive acousmatic works</strong> that use the video game medium to explore new forms of artistic expression.</p>
<h2 id="technical-innovation-in-service-of-art">Technical Innovation in Service of Art</h2>
<p><strong>My works exploit:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Controlled triggering of sound objects</strong>: To experiment with acousmatic phrasing</li>
<li><strong>Adaptive spatialization</strong>: The sound environment adapts to the listener&rsquo;s movements</li>
<li><strong>Interactive polyphony</strong>: Real-time construction of complex textures</li>
<li><strong>Spatial navigation</strong>: Exploration of impossible sound spaces</li>
</ul>
<h2 id="toward-a-new-form-of-acousmatic-art">Toward a New Form of Acousmatic Art</h2>
<p>Interactive acousmatic music doesn&rsquo;t just illustrate action: it <strong>becomes</strong> the action. The listener no longer passively consumes a work, they participate in its real-time creation. This approach questions the boundaries between composer, interpreter, and listener.</p>
<p><strong>The future of this convergence:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Acousmatic virtual reality</strong>: Total immersion in impossible sound universes</li>
<li><strong>Creative artificial intelligence</strong>: Procedural generation of electroacoustic works</li>
<li><strong>Online musical collaboration</strong>: Co-creation of shared sound universes</li>
<li><strong>Interactive installations</strong>: Reactive sound art spaces</li>
</ul>
<h2 id="a-new-artistic-language">A New Artistic Language</h2>
<p>For today&rsquo;s electroacoustic composers, interactivity represents a <strong>new artistic language</strong>. It allows exploring unprecedented forms of composition, creating evolving works, and fundamentally rethinking the relationship between creator and receiver.</p>
<p>This approach doesn&rsquo;t &ldquo;democratize&rdquo; acousmatic music: it <strong>reinvents</strong> it as living and participatory art.</p>
<h2 id="listening-as-performance">Listening as Performance</h2>
<p>In this new form of acousmatic art, <strong>listening becomes performance</strong>. The fineness of sound perception and sensitivity to nuances determine the richness of the artistic experience. The listener develops an intimate relationship with the work, becoming co-creator of their own aesthetic experience.</p>
<p>Interactive acousmatic music thus opens a promising path for the evolution of contemporary sound art, creating bridges between experimental tradition and digital expression.</p>
<hr>
<p><em>Discover my interactive acousmatic works on <a href="https://jcploquin.itch.io">jcploquin.itch.io</a> and explore <strong>for free</strong> the universe of immersive electroacoustic composition.</em></p>

        </div>

                <a href="/en/blog/behind-the-scenes-creation-dascendance/" class="next-article">Behind the Scenes: Creating Ascendance →</a>

    <main>
        
<article class="single-page">
    <div class="container">
        <header class="page-header">
            <h1>Arthur Gyl interviews Jean-Christophe Ploquin</h1>
            <p class="article-meta">7 June 2025</p>
        </header>
        <div class="page-content markdown-content">
            <h2 id="arthur-gyl-interviews-jean-christophe-ploquin">Arthur Gyl interviews Jean-Christophe Ploquin</h2>
<p><strong>Arthur Gyl:</strong> So Jean-Christophe, here we are again! This time, I&rsquo;d like to understand how you create your musical games. Where does this strange idea of mixing music and gameplay come from?</p>
<p><strong>Jean-Christophe Ploquin:</strong> <em>[laughs]</em> Strange, you say? Coming from someone who writes science fiction, that&rsquo;s rich! But seriously, it all started with the acousmatic-plastic exhibition <em>&ldquo;Music &amp; Me&rdquo;</em> that I created with Louis Jeanne in 2022. The audience really appreciated discovering acousmatic music in a playful and interactive way. Then my son Alexandre finished his animation studies at Laniméa and started designing a point-and-click game. That gave me an idea! What if I adapted <em>&ldquo;Music &amp; Me&rdquo;</em> into a video game?</p>
<p><strong>A.G.:</strong> Ah, family influence! But concretely, how do you go from an exhibition with sound sculptures to a video game? It&rsquo;s not the same medium at all!</p>
<p><strong>J-C.P.:</strong> Exactly! That&rsquo;s where it gets exciting. In the exhibition, visitors touched sculptures to trigger sounds, created their own compositions&hellip; The principle was already interactive. For the game, I kept this idea: the player explores a musical universe, but instead of sculptures, they click on visual elements that trigger sound sequences. The music builds up as exploration progresses.</p>
<p><strong>A.G.:</strong> Wait, you&rsquo;re telling me that the music changes according to what the player does? How do you do that technically? Because when I write, my words don&rsquo;t change depending on who reads them!</p>
<p><strong>J-C.P.:</strong> <em>[laughs]</em> Ah, but imagine if your novels adapted to the reader! Actually, I use &ldquo;stems&rdquo; - separate musical layers that I can activate or deactivate in real time. With Godot as the game engine and Ardour for audio, I create musical loops that assemble differently according to the player&rsquo;s actions. It&rsquo;s like having a virtual orchestra that reacts to clicks!</p>
<p><strong>A.G.:</strong> Godot, Ardour&hellip; you&rsquo;re speaking Chinese! But tell me, what was your biggest failure? There must have been moments when it turned into total cacophony?</p>
<p><strong>J-C.P.:</strong> Oh my! <em>[laughs]</em> I remember a bug where all the tracks triggered at the same time instead of alternating. Imagine 15 different musical loops superimposing all at once! It was&hellip; let&rsquo;s say&hellip; very experimental! But sometimes, these accidents give ideas. That bug inspired me to create a sequence where musical chaos is part of the gameplay.</p>
<p><strong>A.G.:</strong> But concretely, what do you start with? The music or the gameplay?</p>
<p><strong>J-C.P.:</strong> The music, of course! The gameplay is just the interactive layer that allows the listener to act on the music. All musical possibilities must be considered beforehand, and validated as musically interesting. It&rsquo;s non-linear writing, composition by reservoirs, where nothing is really left to chance.</p>
<p><strong>A.G.:</strong> What&rsquo;s the biggest challenge? Making music or making a game?</p>
<p><strong>J-C.P.:</strong> They&rsquo;re completely different: both are enormous challenges, each in their own way, but music depends on my creativity while the game depends on my technical skills.</p>
<p><strong>A.G.:</strong> Do you want to educate people about acousmatic music or just entertain them?</p>
<p><strong>J-C.P.:</strong> Thirty years in the national education system and I would continue teaching? No, seriously, I seek to entertain them above all. We say &ldquo;play music&rdquo;: it&rsquo;s a game! We must have fun! If they learn things at the same time, well, so much the better.</p>
<p><strong>A.G.:</strong> And now, you&rsquo;re working on Ascendance&hellip; What&rsquo;s the difference?</p>
<p><strong>J-C.P.:</strong> &ldquo;Ascendance&rdquo; is the adaptation of a play that I never had the pleasure of having performed; a game based on genealogy, another of my obsessions. A tragicomic journey to meet ancestors, also built on non-linear reservoirs. Any family tree can be loaded into the game: the journeys are different each time. But I&rsquo;m not working on it anymore: it&rsquo;s finished and comes out on June 9th. I&rsquo;m already on another project.</p>
<p><strong>A.G.:</strong> But you never stop! It&rsquo;s bulimia! And what will it be this time?</p>
<p><strong>J-C.P.:</strong> &ldquo;GraviTone&rdquo;, an adaptation of the video-musical exhibition &ldquo;Fluctus Planetarium&rdquo;; another of my projects with Louis Jeanne.</p>
<p><strong>A.G.:</strong> And then you stop!</p>
<p><strong>J-C.P.:</strong> Never!</p>
<p><strong>A.G.:</strong> You&rsquo;ll still have to let me write from time to time&hellip; People are asking for the sequel to the homo permanens adventures!</p>
<p><strong>J-C.P.:</strong> Promise. I&rsquo;ll leave you a slot.</p>

        </div>
        
        
        
        <nav class="article-nav">
            
            
                <a href="/en/blog/my-journey-toward-musical-interactivity/" class="next-article">My Journey Toward Musical Interactivity →</a>
            
        </nav>

   <meta charset="UTF-8">
   <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">

window.addEventListener('scroll', function() {
  const aLaUne = document.getElementById('a-la-une');

    aLaUne.classList.add('floating');
    aLaUne.style.top = headerHeight + 'px';
  } else {
    aLaUne.classList.remove('floating');
    aLaUne.style.top = '';
  }
});

    <header class="site-header">
    <div class="container">
        <div class="header-content">
            
            <div class="language-selector-header">
                
                    
                        
                            <a href="/blog/behind-the-scenes-creation-dascendance/" title="Français" class="lang-flag">🇫🇷</a>
                        
                    
                
                
                
                
                    <span class="lang-flag-header current" title="English">🇬🇧</span>
                
            </div>
            
            <h1 class="site-title">
                <div>TWO FACES OF AN ARTIST</div>
            </h1>
            
            <nav class="site-nav">
                <div class="nav-flex">
                    <input type="checkbox" id="menu-toggle"/>
                    <label for="menu-toggle" class="menu-icon">☰</label>
                    <ul class="menu">
                        
                            <li><a href="/en/">Home</a></li>
                        
                            <li><a href="/en/musique/">Music</a></li>
                        
                            <li><a href="/en/litterature/">Literature</a></li>
                        
                            <li><a href="/en/jeux/">Video Games</a></li>
                        
                            <li><a href="/en/about/">Biography</a></li>
                        
                            <li><a href="/en/contact/">Contact</a></li>
                        
                            <li><a href="/en/blog/">Blog</a></li>
                        
                    </ul>
                </div>
            </nav>
        </div>
    </div>
</header>



    
        <div class="banner-slice">
            <img src="/img/banniere-tranche.jpg" alt="Bandeau graphique" class="banner-slice-img">
        </div>
    

    <main>
        
<article class="single-page">
    <div class="container">
        <header class="page-header">
            <h1>Behind the Scenes: Creating Ascendance</h1>
            <p class="article-meta">5 July 2025</p>
        </header>
        <div class="page-content markdown-content">
            <p>Three weeks after the launch of <em>Ascendance</em>, it&rsquo;s time to lift the veil on the behind-the-scenes of this creation. How do you transform an artistic idea into an interactive work? A look back at a creative process that blends electroacoustic composition and software development.</p>
<h2 id="the-creative-spark-when-genealogy-meets-acousmatic-music">The Creative Spark: When Genealogy Meets Acousmatic Music</h2>
<p>The idea for <em>Ascendance</em> was born from a simple observation: <strong>our family trees resemble musical scores</strong>. Each branch evokes a melodic line, each generation suggests a tessitura, each ancestor carries within them a unique sonic story.</p>
<p>This revelation came to me while exploring my own family archives. Contemplating the birth and death dates of my ancestors, I could already hear the bells, choirs, and trumpets that would become the fundamental sound objects of the work.</p>
<h2 id="the-technical-challenge-making-gedcom-and-audio-dialogue">The Technical Challenge: Making GEDCOM and Audio Dialogue</h2>
<p><strong>First major obstacle:</strong> how to make a GEDCOM file &ldquo;speak&rdquo;? These genealogy files contain a wealth of information, but in a purely textual format. I needed to create a <strong>parser</strong> capable of extracting relevant data and transforming it into musical parameters.</p>
<h2 id="what-is-a-parser">What is a Parser?</h2>
<p>A <strong>parser</strong> is a computer program that analyzes a character string and breaks it down into elements understandable by the machine. Etymologically, the term comes from the Latin &ldquo;pars&rdquo; (part): the parser identifies the different parts of a text according to precise rules.</p>
<p>In the context of <em>Ascendance</em>, my GEDCOM parser must:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Identify</strong> each individual in the family tree</li>
<li><strong>Extract</strong> their birth and death dates</li>
<li><strong>Understand</strong> family relationships</li>
<li><strong>Convert</strong> this information into spatial and temporal coordinates for composition</li>
</ul>
<h2 id="the-parallel-with-xenakis-and-stochastic-music">The Parallel with Xenakis and Stochastic Music</h2>
<p>This approach recalls the revolutionary work of Iannis Xenakis in the 1950s. The Greek composer used <strong>stochastic mathematics</strong> - based on probability calculations - to transform abstract data into concrete musical material.</p>
<p><strong>Xenakis transformed:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Laws of physics</strong> (gas kinetics, Markov chains) into <strong>compositional rules</strong></li>
<li><strong>Natural phenomena</strong> (cicada songs, crowd demonstrations) into <strong>sound masses</strong></li>
<li><strong>Probabilistic equations</strong> into <strong>musical events</strong></li>
</ul>
<p><strong>In Ascendance, I transform:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Genealogical data</strong> into <strong>spatialized sound objects</strong></li>
<li><strong>Family links</strong> into <strong>harmonic relationships</strong></li>
<li><strong>Historical dates</strong> into <strong>temporal progressions</strong></li>
</ul>
<p>Like Xenakis who &ldquo;parsed&rdquo; natural phenomena to extract compositional laws, my GEDCOM parser extracts the musical structure hidden in our family histories.</p>
<h2 id="the-sound-architecture-three-universes-three-aesthetics">The Sound Architecture: Three Universes, Three Aesthetics</h2>
<p><em>Ascendance</em> is structured in three distinct movements, each requiring a specific compositional approach:</p>
<h2 id="the-warm-up-journey-through-musical-history">The Warm-up: Journey Through Musical History</h2>
<p><strong>Artistic challenge:</strong> How to represent the passage of historical time through music? I chose to superimpose samples from different eras - Pierre Henry, Léo Delibes, Gregorian chant, ancient music - in random order. This controlled cacophony prepares the listener for the temporal journey ahead.</p>
<h2 id="the-outward-journey-each-ancestor-a-sound-signature">The Outward Journey: Each Ancestor, a Sound Signature</h2>
<p><strong>Technical innovation:</strong> Each ancestor automatically generates three symbolic sound objects. Birth dates trigger trumpets, life periods activate choirs, death dates resonate bells. Quadraphonic spatialization allows perception of only &ldquo;nearby&rdquo; ancestors in the tree.</p>
<h2 id="the-return-journey-the-temporal-avalanche">The Return Journey: The Temporal Avalanche</h2>
<p><strong>Radical aesthetic choice:</strong> Abandoning individual melody for a descending global texture. This &ldquo;chromatic avalanche&rdquo; symbolizes the cascade of generations, creating a striking dramatic effect.</p>
<h2 id="the-challenges-of-interactive-spatialization">The Challenges of Interactive Spatialization</h2>
<p><strong>Complex problem:</strong> How to navigate a sound space too vast to be grasped globally? The solution lay in creating a &ldquo;sonic horizon&rdquo; - only ancestors within a 4-generation radius remain audible.</p>
<p>This technical limitation becomes an artistic choice: it mimics our natural perception of family history, where only &ldquo;close&rdquo; ancestors are truly accessible to us.</p>
<h2 id="harmonic-evolution-the-comma-as-temporal-guide">Harmonic Evolution: The Comma as Temporal Guide</h2>
<p><strong>Compositional discovery:</strong> Rather than progression by semitones (too brutal), I opted for ascending by commas (ninth of a tone). This micro-interval creates a subtle harmonic progression that accompanies genealogical exploration without dominating it.</p>
<p>Each generation ascended slightly raises the overall pitch, creating a sensation of spiritual elevation toward origins.</p>
<h2 id="creation-tools-between-tradition-and-innovation">Creation Tools: Between Tradition and Innovation</h2>
<p><strong>Software palette:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Ardour</strong>: Main sequencer for assembly and multichannel mixing</li>
<li><strong>Pure Data</strong>: Programming musical algorithms and real-time processing</li>
<li><strong>Godot Engine</strong>: Game engine for interactivity and interface</li>
<li><strong>Custom audio libraries</strong>: Sound objects composed specifically for the project</li>
<li><strong>GEDCOM parsing scripts</strong>: Developed custom for genealogical analysis</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Pure Data</strong> plays a central role in my creations. This visual programming language allows me to develop the complex musical algorithms that transform genealogical data into sound objects. Each ancestor, each date, each family link passes through Pure Data patches that calculate spatialization, pitch, and timbre parameters in real time.</p>
<h2 id="user-experience-when-art-meets-its-audience">User Experience: When Art Meets Its Audience</h2>
<p>Since launch, feedback reveals unexpected uses. Some users explore <em>Ascendance</em> as a family meditation tool, others find a new way to understand their heritage. This diversity of approaches confirms the richness of the interactive medium.</p>
<p><strong>Notable testimonials:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>&ldquo;I finally &lsquo;heard&rsquo; my great-grandfather&rdquo;</li>
<li>&ldquo;An overwhelming experience that gives a sonic dimension to my genealogy&rdquo;</li>
<li>&ldquo;More than a game, it&rsquo;s an inner journey&rdquo;</li>
</ul>
<h2 id="toward-new-creative-horizons">Toward New Creative Horizons</h2>
<p><em>Ascendance</em> opens the way to other experiments. The idea of adapting this approach to other types of data - geographical, historical, scientific - is already germinating. The principle remains the same: transform information into immersive artistic experience.</p>
<h2 id="lessons-learned">Lessons Learned</h2>
<p><strong>Artistically:</strong> Interactivity must never dominate the aesthetic dimension. Every technical choice must serve the musical intention.</p>
<p><strong>Technically:</strong> Simplicity of use often hides development complexity. Three clicks to load a GEDCOM represent hundreds of hours of programming.</p>
<p><strong>Humanly:</strong> Interactive art creates unique links between creator and audience. Each user becomes co-creator of their own experience.</p>
<p><em>Ascendance</em> demonstrates that it&rsquo;s possible to rethink the boundaries between composition, programming, and user experience. This hybrid work opens new perspectives for 21st-century sound art, following in the footsteps of Xenakis&rsquo;s innovations that already transformed mathematics into music.</p>
<hr>
<p><em>Discover Ascendance for free on <a href="https://jcploquin.itch.io/ascendance">jcploquin.itch.io/ascendance</a> and explore your own musical genealogical journey.</em></p>

        </div>
        
        
        
        <nav class="article-nav">
            
                <a href="/en/blog/lacousmatique-dans-les-jeux-video/" class="prev-article">← Acousmatic Music in Video Games</a>
            
            
                <a href="/en/blog/cranelab-2025/" class="next-article">Review of the 2025 Acousmatic Meetings at CRANE lab →</a>
            
        </nav>
        
    </div>
</article>

    </main>

<article class="single-page">
    <div class="container">
        <header class="page-header">
            <h1>Hydrophonia - remix (2025): Aquatic Poem and the Anthropocene</h1>
            <p class="article-meta">16 July 2025</p>
        </header>
        <div class="page-content markdown-content">
            <h1 id="hydrophonia---remix-2025">Hydrophonia - remix (2025)</h1>
<p><em>Aquatic poem and Anthropocene crisis</em></p>
<h2 id="overview">Overview</h2>
<p><em>Hydrophonia - remix (2025)</em> is an electroacoustic poem tracing the journey of water in all its forms, <strong>from its cosmic emergence to the catastrophe of the Anthropocene</strong>. The piece explores:</p>
<ul>
<li>the genesis of water,</li>
<li>its secret flows,</li>
<li>its metamorphoses,</li>
<li>its real and mythical landscapes,</li>
<li>up to the contamination and destruction caused by modern humanity.</li>
</ul>
<p>This project offers an <strong>immersive listening experience of our contemporary ecological crisis</strong>, through the lens of the primordial element—water.</p>
<h2 id="genesis-and-versions">Genesis and Versions</h2>
<p>This work was <strong>originally composed for octophonic diffusion</strong>, tailored to the acousmonium setup of the Apnées festival (PAYSAGES | COMPOSÉS 2025), allowing full spatialization on eight speakers.<br>
<em>The version presented here is a stereophonic reduction, specially adapted for YouTube.</em><br>
<strong>Headphone listening is strongly recommended</strong> to fully experience the richness and inner movement of the sound spaces.</p>
<h2 id="context-and-current-status">Context and Current Status</h2>
<p>This composition was <strong>submitted for the <a href="https://apnees.wordpress.com/2025/06/04/appel-a-oeuvres-paysages-composes-2025/">PAYSAGES | COMPOSÉS 2025 call for works</a></strong>, organized by Apnées.<br>
<em>Selection decision pending.</em></p>
<h2 id="a-sonic-and-poetic-journey">A Sonic and Poetic Journey</h2>
<p><em>Hydrophonia - remix</em> invites the listener on a sonic journey:<br>
<em>A mirror for both our mythologies and our excesses, water transforms from a primordial flow and force of life into a victim of human activity in the age of the Anthropocene.</em></p>
<p><strong><a href="https://youtu.be/mFXsNs8Gd4Y">Video coming soon</a>, Thursday, July 17 at 11:00 AM — share your impressions after listening!</strong></p>
<h2 id="a-unique-visual-perspective-bruno-maurey">A Unique Visual Perspective: Bruno Maurey</h2>
<p>The images in this video are drawn from <strong>unreleased footage provided by Bruno Maurey</strong>, a photographer and video artist whose visual poetry explores:</p>
<blockquote>
<p>&ldquo;light, slowness, the boundary between radiance and fading.&rdquo;</p>
</blockquote>
<p>His search for the <em>&ldquo;passage of time within the image&rdquo;</em> resonates naturally with the aquatic, ever-shifting narrative of the piece.</p>
<blockquote>
<p>&ldquo;Capturing light, casting slowness into reality.&rdquo;</p>
</blockquote>
<p>Thank you to Bruno Maurey for this rare visual material, an invitation to:</p>
<blockquote>
<p>&ldquo;explore what resists, trembles, or dissolves&rdquo; —<br>
an alchemy of image and sound.</p>
</blockquote>
<hr>
<p><em>Follow the release of the video on <a href="https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL_oknYk97Qe0YRPFwkhjDLRhDe58xNE2N">my YouTube channel</a>, discover all my music-gaming creations at <a href="https://jcploquin.itch.io">jcploquin.itch.io</a>, and dive into the visual universe of <a href="https://www.brunomaurey.com/">Bruno Maurey</a>.</em></p>

    <main>
        
<article class="single-page">
    <div class="container">
        <header class="page-header">
            <h1>What is Acousmatic Music?</h1>
            <p class="article-meta">25 June 2025</p>
        </header>
        <div class="page-content markdown-content">
            <p>Acousmatic music remains one of the most mysterious and fascinating currents in contemporary sound creation. But what lies behind this enigmatic term?</p>
<h2 id="the-art-of-blind-listening">The Art of Blind Listening</h2>
<p>The term &ldquo;acousmatic&rdquo; has its roots in ancient Greece. Pythagoras taught his disciples hidden behind a curtain, deliberately depriving them of any visual information to focus their attention solely on speech. This &ldquo;blind&rdquo; listening allowed for a deeper understanding of the message.</p>
<p>Pierre Schaeffer, pioneer of musique concrète in the 1940s, revived this concept to define a revolutionary musical art: <em>Acousmatic listening invites us to discover sounds for themselves, freed from their origin</em>.</p>
<h2 id="when-sound-becomes-pure-music">When Sound Becomes Pure Music</h2>
<p>In my practice as an electroacoustic composer[3], acousmatic music represents that particular magic where a familiar sound - the noise of water, the breath of wind, the resonance of an object - transforms into pure musical material. The composer becomes a sculptor of the invisible, shaping soundscapes that exist only in the space of listening.</p>
<p><strong>Acousmatic music is:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>The art of making the unheard audible in everyday life</li>
<li>Music that exists only in diffusion, without visible performers</li>
<li>A sonic journey where the listener becomes an explorer of imaginary universes</li>
</ul>
<h2 id="the-immersive-listening-experience">The Immersive Listening Experience</h2>
<p>Unlike traditional instrumental music, acousmatic music immerses us in a total sonic bath. Loudspeakers become windows opening onto impossible worlds. In my compositions like <em>Hydrophonia</em>, this approach allows creating aquatic environments where the listener can literally &ldquo;dive&rdquo; into the sonic universe.</p>
<h2 id="music-of-the-21st-century">Music of the 21st Century</h2>
<p>Today, acousmatic music evolves toward new forms of interactivity. My projects like <em>Ascendance</em> or <em>Music &amp; Me</em> explore how this tradition of pure listening can reinvent itself in the digital era, creating experiences where the listener becomes an actor in their own sonic discovery.</p>
<p><strong>Modern acousmatic music is:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Digital tools serving imagination</li>
<li>Integration of interactivity into contemplative listening</li>
<li>A pedagogical approach that makes contemporary music accessible</li>
</ul>
<h2 id="why-listen-to-acousmatic-music">Why Listen to Acousmatic Music?</h2>
<p>Acousmatic music develops our listening capacity and imagination. It teaches us to hear the hidden music in our daily environment and invites us to rediscover the sonic world that surrounds us.</p>
<p>In a world saturated with images, acousmatic music offers us a space of freedom where only the richness of our inner listening matters.</p>
<hr>
<p><em>As an electroacoustic composer passionate about transmission, I develop interactive tools to make the universe of acousmatic music accessible. Discover my creations at <a href="https://jcploquin.itch.io">jcploquin.itch.io</a>.</em></p>

        </div>
        
        
        
        <nav class="article-nav">
            
                <a href="/en/blog/my-journey-toward-musical-interactivity/" class="prev-article">← My Journey Toward Musical Interactivity</a>

        </nav>

    <main>
        
<article class="single-page">
    <div class="container">
        <header class="page-header">
            <h1>Breaking Language Barriers: How My French SF Trilogy Found Its English Voice</h1>
            <p class="article-meta">14 July 2025</p>
        </header>
        <div class="page-content markdown-content">
            <h1 id="breaking-language-barriers-how-my-french-sf-trilogy-found-its-english-voice">Breaking Language Barriers: How My French SF Trilogy Found Its English Voice</h1>
<p>As an acousmatic music composer, I&rsquo;ve always been fascinated by transformation—how sound can evolve, morph, and transcend its original form. This same fascination with metamorphosis led me to explore a profound question in science fiction: what happens when humanity itself undergoes transformation? Today, I&rsquo;m thrilled to announce that my French science fiction trilogy <strong>&lsquo;De la Mort à la Vie&rsquo;</strong> is now available worldwide in English as <strong>&lsquo;From Death to Life.'</strong></p>
<h2 id="the-art-of-translation">The Art of Translation</h2>
<p>Translating science fiction isn&rsquo;t just about converting words—it&rsquo;s about preserving the philosophical essence while making complex concepts accessible to new cultural contexts. The journey from French <em>&lsquo;De la Mort à la Vie&rsquo;</em> to English <em>&lsquo;From Death to Life&rsquo;</em> required the same meticulous attention to detail I apply to my acousmatic compositions.</p>
<h2 id="a-unique-vision-of-evolution">A Unique Vision of Evolution</h2>
<p>Unlike traditional science fiction that relies on technological consciousness transfer, <strong>&lsquo;From Death to Life&rsquo;</strong> explores spontaneous biological metamorphosis. The Homo Permanens don&rsquo;t achieve immortality through machines—they evolve naturally, transcending death through organic transformation.</p>
<p>This trilogy questions the very nature of human evolution. When death becomes merely a transition, what defines our humanity? How do we create meaning in a post-mortal existence?</p>
<h2 id="available-worldwide">Available Worldwide</h2>
<p><strong>&lsquo;From Death to Life&rsquo;</strong> is now available worldwide through all major digital platforms. Whether you prefer Kobo, Apple Books, Amazon, Barnes &amp; Noble, or any of the 17 international distributors, you can discover the Homo Permanens universe with a single universal link:</p>
<p><strong><a href="https://books2read.com/from-death-to-life-arthur-gyl">📚 Read Now - All Platforms</a></strong></p>
<h2 id="join-the-evolution">Join the Evolution</h2>
<p>The Homo Permanens await readers worldwide. Whether you&rsquo;re discovering my work for the first time or following my transmedia journey from acousmatic compositions to interactive games to science fiction, <strong>&lsquo;From Death to Life&rsquo;</strong> represents the culmination of years exploring what it means to transcend our limitations—whether in sound, in story, or in the very essence of human existence.</p>
<p><strong><a href="https://books2read.com/from-death-to-life-arthur-gyl">🌍 Discover the Universe</a></strong></p>
<h2 id="join-the-evolution-discover-what-lies-beyond-death"><em>Join the evolution. Discover what lies beyond death.</em></h2>
<p><em>Discover my <strong>video game creations</strong> on <a href="https://jcploquin.itch.io">jcploquin.itch.io</a>, explore my <strong>concert pieces and installations</strong> on <a href="https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL_oknYk97Qe0YRPFwkhjDLRhDe58xNE2N">YouTube</a>, and find my <strong>French novels in print</strong> at <a href="https://www.coollibri.com/bibliotheque-en-ligne/arthur-gyl">Coollibri</a>.</em></p>

        </div>
        
        
        
        <nav class="article-nav">

        </nav>

window.addEventListener('scroll', function() {
  const aLaUne = document.getElementById('a-la-une');
  const header = document.querySelector('.site-header');
  if (!aLaUne || !header) return;
  const headerHeight = header.offsetHeight;
  const scrollY = window.scrollY;

  if (scrollY < 100) {
    aLaUne.classList.add('floating');
    aLaUne.style.top = headerHeight + 'px';
  } else {
    aLaUne.classList.remove('floating');
    aLaUne.style.top = '';
  }
});
</script>

            <div class="language-selector-header">
                
                    
                        
                            <a href="/blog/les-coulisses-de-la-cr%C3%A9ation-du-tome-4-du-r%C3%AAve-%C3%A0-la-r%C3%A9alit%C3%A9/" title="Français" class="lang-flag">🇫🇷</a>

        </div>
    

    <main>
        
<article class="single-page">
    <div class="container">
        <header class="page-header">
            <h1>Behind the Scenes of Book 4: From Dream to Reality</h1>
            <p class="article-meta">6 September 2025</p>
        </header>
        <div class="page-content markdown-content">
            <h1 id="behind-the-scenes-of-book-4-from-dream-to-reality">Behind the Scenes of Book 4: From Dream to Reality</h1>
<h2 id="why-a-book-4">Why a Book 4?</h2>
<p>It’s true, after all, wasn’t the story supposed to end at the conclusion of Book 3?<br>
As a true Rouennais, I’ll answer “Maybe yes, maybe no!” The most attentive among you will have noticed that mysterious message at the end of Book 3, “Les Triades”:<br>
<em>&ldquo;Hello, Ace of Hearts? Ace of Clubs here. You’ll never believe what’s happening…&quot;</em></p>
<p>But I know you, dear readers, had already figured it out: something else was going to happen! Is that what Book 4 is about? Not quite… Since it’s “incredible”, the story must build up to make us believe it. So let’s go back to the start!</p>
<hr>
<div style="display: flex; gap: 20px; margin: 20px 0;">
    <img src="/img/Kursad_couv.png" alt="Full wrap-around cover" style="width: 100%; height: auto;" />
    <p style="text-align: center; font-style: italic; margin-top: 10px;">"Kürşad" cover</p>
</div>
<h2 id="reveal-book-4-is-not-a-chronological-sequel-to-book-1">Reveal: Book 4 is NOT a chronological sequel to Book 1!</h2>
<p>So here we are, back where it all began: Earth, at the end of the 21st century, when communications broke down between Adam and his scientist friends in Chooz. I’m going to tell you what happened—not after Book 3, but during the first three books, from another perspective.</p>
<p>Thus, Book 4 is the first in a parallel trilogy that recounts the Earthly adventures leading to the final answer. You’ll find some old friends returning, and you’ll meet many new characters—including, of course, those ultimately responsible for all this horror: Ad Vitam. It was a true pleasure to reunite with my old writing companions, breathe new life into them, and let them experience new adventures.</p>
<hr>
<h2 id="is-it-going-to-last-even-longer">Is it going to last even longer?</h2>
<p>Books 1, 2, and 3 form Adam’s cycle. Since we’re rewinding to the chronological beginning, Books 4, 5, and 6 will form a second cycle. Both cycles will then merge in Books 7, 8, and 9, which will be the final cycle.<br>
But… will that really be the end?</p>
<hr>
<h2 id="unavoidable-challenges">Unavoidable challenges</h2>
<h3 id="keeping-the-chronology-straight">Keeping the chronology straight</h3>
<p>Since Book 1 was written and published four years ago, there was no way I could deviate by even an inch from what’s already been told! I had to create cross-reference tables—so complex, they gave me a fair share of headaches. At one point, I thought everything was fine (about halfway through the story) until I realized there was a 20-year time shift! After the initial panic, I had to start over from the beginning.</p>
<h3 id="planting-seeds">Planting seeds</h3>
<p>You surely know me by now: I love planting seemingly trivial bits of information that only come to fruition much later—sometimes the very next book, sometimes even later! I went back to all these little seeds planted in Book 1 to see them blossom in Book 4. I just love gardening with words! Of course, the seeds that will bloom in Books 5 and 6 are, in some cases, already sown.</p>
<h3 id="fear-of-the-future">Fear of the future</h3>
<p>It won’t surprise you to learn I’m terrified by the current geopolitical events: the rise of fascism, ultra-liberalism, climate change… So many things feed my anxieties, and by extension, my writing. I am afraid of the future. Writing a dystopia set in the coming century is my way of imagining what could come for all of us—for better or worse. Yet, I remain optimistic about humanity’s ability to do good… even when it’s ever rarer.</p>
<h3 id="geography-and-societies">Geography and societies</h3>
<p>I carried out a huge research effort on sub-Saharan Africa: countries, religions, customs… and while it certainly made my work more difficult, I hope it has also enhanced the story’s credibility. Again, I fear the worst for the people living in these regions: instead of receding, colonialism is tightening its grip, and no major powers are helping—quite the opposite. But I know them to be resilient and courageous, so perhaps they will save themselves!</p>
<hr>
<h2 id="many-references-and-stylistic-borrowings">Many references and stylistic borrowings</h2>
<p>I won’t explain them—you’ll have to find them yourselves. But in no particular order: Dick, Verhoeven, Asimov, Herbert, Bordage, Verne…</p>
<hr>
<p><strong>As for the title &ldquo;Kürşad,&rdquo; don’t bother insisting: I won’t say a word!</strong></p>
<hr>
<p><strong>📚 Discover the &ldquo;From Death to Life&rdquo; saga:</strong></p>
<p><strong>🇫🇷 French editions:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong><a href="https://www.coollibri.com/bibliotheque-en-ligne/arthur-gyl">Coollibri</a></strong> – Paperback, 148x210 mm format</li>
<li><strong><a href="https://talentdauteur.fr/arthur-gyl/">Talent d&rsquo;Auteur</a></strong> – ePub 2.0 versions</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>🇬🇧 English edition:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong><a href="https://books2read.com/from-death-to-life-arthur-gyl">Books2Read</a></strong> – &ldquo;From Death to Life&rdquo; available worldwide, ePub 2.0, on: <em>Odilo, Smashwords, Gardners, Everand, BorrowBox, Kobo, Tolino, Vivlio, Barnes &amp; Noble, Apple, Baker &amp; Taylor, OverDrive, Palace Marketplace, Fable, Amazon, cloudLibrary, and Hoopla</em></li>
</ul>
<p><em>As an acousmatic music composer and science fiction writer, I explore, under the pen name <strong><a href="https://www.facebook.com/profile.php?id=61555962083977">Arthur GYL</a></strong>, the borders between sonic universes and forward-looking tales.</em></p>
<p><strong>📦 Attention: The number of paperback copies of &ldquo;Kürşad&rdquo; (french version only) is very limited! If you want to secure your copy, <a href="http://jcploquin.free.fr/en/contact">reserve it now via the ‘Contact’ page</a> or by private message on social media. First come, first served — don’t wait! Official release on September 16th.</strong></p>
<hr>

        </div>

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
	<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=Exo:400,700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
   <meta charset="UTF-8">

<article class="single-page">
    <div class="container">
        <header class="page-header">
            <h1>What if... immortality came with a price? - Volume 4 Teaser: The Rise of Homo Permanens</h1>
            <p class="article-meta">20 July 2025</p>
        </header>
        <div class="page-content markdown-content">
            <h1 id="what-if-immortality-came-with-a-price">What if&hellip; immortality came with a price?</h1>
<h2 id="volume-4-teaser-the-rise-of-homo-permanens">Volume 4 Teaser: The Rise of Homo Permanens</h2>
<h3 id="previous-volumes-recap">Previous volumes recap</h3>
<p><strong>Volume 1:</strong> An ordinary man crosses the ultimate frontier and embarks on an initiatory journey that takes him to the edge of his identity and the galaxy. Questions of passage, memory, and becoming take center stage.</p>
<p><strong>Volume 2:</strong> Several generations later, two singular beings face the mysteries of a new environment where exploration of the world, life, and origins takes an unexpected turn. Love, lineage, and transmission intertwine with the quest for meaning.</p>
<p><strong>Volume 3:</strong> The universe expands: new powers emerge, society changes, but each character&rsquo;s destiny remains woven from intimate choices and collective upheavals.</p>
<p>Each volume reveals a facet of humanity confronted with the extraordinary, questioning life, death, mutation, and time without ever imposing a single truth.</p>
<h3 id="the-world-has-changed">The world has changed&hellip;</h3>
<p>While Adam explores the mysteries of Gliese, terrestrial humanity is shifting.
Ad Vitam no longer offers—it imposes.</p>
<h3 id="a-new-species-emerges">A new species emerges</h3>
<p><strong>Homo Permanens: evolution or abdication?</strong></p>
<p>As Adam left Earth for the planet Gliese, leaving behind a humanity in full mutation, a new upheaval looms&hellip;</p>
<p>Ad Vitam, an organization promising eternal life, gradually extends its grip: from individual choices, we shift toward a system where transmutation becomes the norm.</p>
<p>A new type of humanity emerges: homo permanens.</p>
<p>But in this world where death is nothing more than an old memory, can individual freedom survive the collective will to immortality?</p>
<p><em>Stay connected to follow the birth of homo permanens&hellip;</em></p>
<h3 id="the-game-changing-question">The game-changing question</h3>
<p>What if the real suspense wasn&rsquo;t about immortality, but about what we&rsquo;re willing to give up to achieve it?</p>
<hr>
<p><strong>Keywords:</strong> Science-Fiction • Homo Permanens • Ad Vitam • Immortality • Saga • Teaser • Novel in Progress</p>
<p><strong>Themes:</strong> Transhumanism • Speculative Literature • SciFi Philosophy • Ethics and Technology</p>
<hr>
<p><strong>📚 Discover the &ldquo;From Death to Life&rdquo; saga:</strong></p>
<p><strong>🇫🇷 French editions:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong><a href="https://www.coollibri.com/bibliotheque-en-ligne/arthur-gyl">Coollibri</a></strong> - Paperback versions, 148x210 mm format</li>
<li><strong><a href="https://talentdauteur.fr/arthur-gyl/">Talent d&rsquo;Auteur</a></strong> - ePub 2.0 versions</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>🇬🇧 English edition:</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong><a href="https://books2read.com/from-death-to-life-arthur-gyl">Books2Read</a></strong> - &ldquo;From Death to Life&rdquo; available worldwide, ePub 2.0, on: <em>Odilo, Smashwords, Gardners, Everand, BorrowBox, Kobo, Tolino, Vivlio, Barnes &amp; Noble, Apple, Baker &amp; Taylor, OverDrive, Palace Marketplace, Fable,</em> and soon on <em>Amazon, cloudLibrary, and Hoopla</em></li>
</ul>
<p><em>As an acousmatic music composer and science fiction writer, I explore under the pen name <strong><a href="https://www.facebook.com/profile.php?id=61555962083977">Arthur GYL</a></strong> the boundaries between sonic universes and speculative narratives.</em></p>
<p><strong>➡️ Volume 4 coming soon <em>(title yet to be determined)</em>&hellip; Stay connected!</strong></p>

    <main>
        
<article class="single-page">
    <div class="container">
        <header class="page-header">
            <h1>My Journey Toward Musical Interactivity</h1>
            <p class="article-meta">13 June 2025</p>
        </header>
        <div class="page-content markdown-content">
            <p>My path toward creating interactive musical works hasn&rsquo;t been a straight line. Between guitar, electroacoustic music, and digital technology, it&rsquo;s a journey made of determining encounters and sometimes unexpected discoveries that led me to explore these new artistic territories.</p>
<h2 id="the-determining-influence-of-francis-faber">The Determining Influence of Francis Faber</h2>
<p><strong>Francis Faber, my former teacher, planted the first seeds</strong> of my fascination with interactive art through his installation <strong>&ldquo;Totem&rdquo;</strong>. This revolutionary work made me understand that music could transcend the traditional concert framework to become an immersive and participatory experience.</p>
<p>&ldquo;Totem&rdquo; already proposed this revolutionary idea: the listener no longer endures the work, they shape it through their presence and gestures. This discovery profoundly marked my conception of musical creation and opened my reflection on the possibilities offered by interactivity.</p>
<h2 id="from-guitar-to-electroacoustic-music">From Guitar to Electroacoustic Music</h2>
<p>My journey began with guitar, with several albums produced in collaboration with Thomas Demarle at Musicopré. This traditional studio experience gave me a solid technical foundation, but I aspired to explore other sonic territories.</p>
<p>The transition to electroacoustic composition happened naturally, nourished by my curiosity for contemporary composers and new musical technologies. Pure Data became my tool of choice for developing musical algorithms, while Ardour allowed me to structure my multichannel compositions.</p>
<h2 id="the-revelation-smiles-from-ear-to-ear">The Revelation: Smiles from Ear to Ear</h2>
<p><strong>The decisive moment</strong> came during the first public presentations of <em>Musique &amp; Moi</em>. I expected a polite but distant reception - after all, interactive acousmatic music isn&rsquo;t exactly mainstream.</p>
<p><strong>Reality stunned me.</strong> The users, though novices in electroacoustic music, displayed <strong>smiles from ear to ear</strong>. They discovered with wonder that they could &ldquo;play&rdquo; music without prior knowledge, experiment with acousmatic phrasing through simple manipulation of sound objects.</p>
<h2 id="an-unexpected-contagious-joy">An Unexpected Contagious Joy</h2>
<p><strong>The enthusiasm was such</strong> that I had to postpone the exhibition&rsquo;s closing time. Visitors didn&rsquo;t want to leave! They chained sessions, moving from solo to duo, then trio and quartet, progressively discovering the richness of acousmatic polyphony.</p>
<p>This reaction deeply marked me. It revealed that interactivity could make accessible a music considered difficult, create bridges between contemporary art and the general public. <strong>The interface becomes mediation</strong>, the playful experience opens doors to aesthetic discovery.</p>
<h2 id="understanding-this-enthusiastic-reception">Understanding This Enthusiastic Reception</h2>
<p><strong>Why such success with novices?</strong> Several factors explain this reception:</p>
<p><strong>Learning through experience:</strong> Rather than theoretically explaining acousmatic composition, <em>Musique &amp; Moi</em> allows <strong>living</strong> it directly. Users intuitively understand concepts through manipulation.</p>
<p><strong>Pedagogical progression:</strong> From solo to quartet, the work naturally guides toward complexity, never losing the user.</p>
<p><strong>Absence of judgment:</strong> Unlike a traditional instrument, there are no possible &ldquo;wrong notes.&rdquo; Each interaction generates sonic beauty.</p>
<h2 id="toward-new-horizons">Toward New Horizons</h2>
<p>These enthusiastic responses confirmed the relevance of my artistic approach. They encouraged me to continue with <em>Ascendance</em>, this time exploring spatialization through genealogical exploration.</p>
<p><strong>Each interactive work</strong> thus becomes a laboratory of human as well as musical experiences. It reveals unsuspected listening potentials, creates moments of shared joy around sonic discovery.</p>
<h2 id="interactive-art-as-revealer">Interactive Art as Revealer</h2>
<p>My journey taught me that <strong>interactive art possesses this unique capacity</strong> to reveal everyone&rsquo;s musical sensitivity. It democratizes access to complex aesthetics while preserving their artistic richness.</p>
<p>These smiles from ear to ear remain my most beautiful reward. They confirm that technology, serving a clear artistic vision, can create profoundly human and joyful experiences.</p>
<hr>
<p><em>Discover my interactive acousmatic works on <a href="https://jcploquin.itch.io">jcploquin.itch.io</a> and live your own musical creation experience.</em></p>

        </div>

<article class="single-page">
    <div class="container">
        <header class="page-header">
            <h1>🔎 Quick Update: A Look at Three Projects</h1>
            <p class="article-meta">22 July 2025</p>
        </header>
        <div class="page-content markdown-content">
            <h2 id="hydrophonia--remix-2025">Hydrophonia – Remix 2025</h2>
<p>The 9-minute stereo version of <em>Hydrophonia – Remix 2025</em> is now available <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mFXsNs8Gd4Y">on YouTube</a>.<br>
At the same time, the piece has been submitted to <strong>PAYSAGES | COMPOSÉS 2025</strong>, the call for experimental sound works organised by <strong>APNÉES</strong>. Jury results are expected at the end of August.</p>
<h2 id="volume-4-of-from-death-to-life">Volume 4 of <em>From Death to Life</em></h2>
<p>Writing continues: the manuscript has just passed the 7,000-word mark.<br>
The <strong>title is still to be decided</strong>, but the overall structure is locked in:</p>
<ul>
<li>novella format, like the first three volumes (≈32,000 words)</li>
<li>3 main acts broken into roughly ten chapters</li>
<li>several interludes titled “Voices of Homo Permanens”<br>
Target for the first full read-through: <strong>October 2025</strong>.</li>
</ul>
<h2 id="game--fluctus-planetarium">Game – Fluctus Planetarium</h2>
<p>The game adaptation of <strong>“Fluctus Planetarium”</strong> has entered the polishing phase:</p>
<ul>
<li>event-card balancing was completed last week</li>
<li>rulebook revision now at v1.12.3</li>
<li>prototypes printed for early-August blind-play tests</li>
</ul>
<blockquote>
<p>A <strong>stable release</strong> is pencilled in for <strong>September 2025</strong>, provided the final tests go smoothly.</p>
</blockquote>
<hr>
<p>Next check-in at the end of summer—hopefully with good news from APNÉES and the definitive title for Volume 4!</p>
